prose:
Method–provenance summary (reproducible):

• Property: atom‐condensed Fukui indices f⁺ (electrophilic), f⁻ (nucleophilic), f⁰ (radical) and dual descriptor for benzaldehyde; most electrophilic and most nucleophilic sites identified.

• Level of theory: DFT, functional B3LYP with def2-TZVP basis (tier ‘standard’), no density fitting (exact four-center integrals) in PySCF 2.5.0 via chemkit.

• Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (benzaldehyde.xyz); single-point calculations for the neutral (N), cation (N–1), and anion (N+1) states — no additional geometry optimization (notation: DFT//input XYZ).

• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).

• Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet parent (charge 0, multiplicity 1); cation and anion states were doublets (charge ±1, multiplicity 2).

• Convergence & integrity: all three SCFs converged; integrity.status = ok; integrity.trustworthy = true.  Three engine warnings (see below) note charge-conservation checks and schema header.

Key numerical results (Mulliken partitioning):
 Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (C, the carbonyl carbon) f⁺ = 0.39938964  
 Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (O) f⁻ = 0.23839906  

Live run log was written during execution (tailable in real time) to the standard chemkit `<cwd>/fukui_<timestamp>.out`; full JSON result saved as `benzaldehyde_fukui_b3lyp_def2tzvp2.json` (see cli_invocation inside the JSON for exact command).

Verbatim warnings from chemkit:
1. sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)  
2. sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)  
3. common header present  

The calculation therefore meets internal integrity checks and is flagged trustworthy.
